From: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: j.henderson@techsupplier.ca
Subject: Network Switch Delivery — Timeline Confirmation
Date: March 6, 2026

Jason,

Following up on your message from March 2 — can you please provide an
updated delivery commitment for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches?

Specifically, we need to know:
1. The confirmed delivery date for the Gatineau (Site A) order of 24 units.
2. Whether the BGP routing modules (C9500-NM-2Q) are included in the
   revised shipment.

extracted_tasks.text: Provide the tracking number once the shipment is dispatched.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: The tracking number once the shipment is dispatched from your warehouse.

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Minimum of 5 business days required for hardware staging and testing prior to migration.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: we need a minimum of 5 business days for hardware staging and testing before the migration window opens.

```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments",
    "Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024",
    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024",
    "D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due January 31, 2025",
    "D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025",
    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act",
    "Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process",
    "Scope changes require written approval from the Project Sponsor and must be assessed for cost and schedule impact before implementation"
  ]
}
```

out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025

D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026

D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.97,
  "drift_type": "within_scope",
  "detected_ask": "Confirmation of delivery date, BGP routing module inclusion (C9500-NM-2Q), and tracking number for Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches destined for Site A (Gatineau), with a minimum 5-business-day staging and pre-configuration window before migration.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; BGP routing table migration and validation; Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A; D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026",
  "suggested_action": "No corrective action required. Project Manager should log the delivery commitment response in the procurement tracking register and update the integrated master schedule to reflect the confirmed hardware arrival date. If the confirmed delivery date compresses the 5-business-day staging window to fewer than 5 days before the migration window, escalate immediately as a schedule risk against D-006 (Go-Live, April 30, 2026). Verify that the C9500-NM-2Q BGP routing modules are captured on the existing purchase order to avoid any unplanned procurement that could affect the approved $2,400,000 CAD budget.",
  "reasoning": "The email is a routine procurement follow-up entirely consistent with the approved scope. The Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment is explicitly in scope, and the BGP routing modules (C9500-NM-2Q) are a standard line-card component directly supporting the in-scope BGP routing table migration and validation activity. The request for a delivery timeline and tracking number is normal contract administration. The only latent risk is schedule sensitivity: with Go-Live fixed at April 30, 2026, any hardware delivery delay that erodes the 5-business-day staging buffer could threaten D-006 and trigger downstream impacts on D-007 (Post-Migration Validation Report, May 31, 2026). No new requirements, budget implications, or out-of-scope activities are introduced by this document."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Confirmation of delivery date, BGP routing module inclusion (C9500-NM-2Q), and tracking number for Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches destined for Site A (Gatineau), with a minimum 5-business-day staging and pre-configuration window before migration.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; BGP routing table migration and validation; Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A; D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026
parsed_json.suggested_action: No corrective action required. Project Manager should log the delivery commitment response in the procurement tracking register and update the integrated master schedule to reflect the confirmed hardware arrival date. If the confirmed delivery date compresses the 5-business-day staging window to fewer than 5 days before the migration window, escalate immediately as a schedule risk against D-006 (Go-Live, April 30, 2026). Verify that the C9500-NM-2Q BGP routing modules are captured on the existing purchase order to avoid any unplanned procurement that could affect the approved $2,400,000 CAD budget.
parsed_json.reasoning: The email is a routine procurement follow-up entirely consistent with the approved scope. The Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment is explicitly in scope, and the BGP routing modules (C9500-NM-2Q) are a standard line-card component directly supporting the in-scope BGP routing table migration and validation activity. The request for a delivery timeline and tracking number is normal contract administration. The only latent risk is schedule sensitivity: with Go-Live fixed at April 30, 2026, any hardware delivery delay that erodes the 5-business-day staging buffer could threaten D-006 and trigger downstream impacts on D-007 (Post-Migration Validation Report, May 31, 2026). No new requirements, budget implications, or out-of-scope activities are introduced by this document.
